items.title: KFF: States gearing up for major Medicaid/CHIP rule changes starting Oct. 2026 and Jan. 2027
items.summary: A new KFF/Georgetown survey says states are preparing for Medicaid eligibility restrictions for certain lawfully residing immigrants starting Oct. 1, 2026, along with new requirements for ACA expansion adults starting Jan. 1, 2027 (including work/community engagement requirements and 6-month renewals). The report also flags future changes like mandatory cost-sharing (up to $35 per service) for some expansion adults beginning Oct. 2028.

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits for federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS released guidance (via a State Health Official letter) to prepare states for a statutory change that limits when states can claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for people who are not U.S. citizens/nationals or who fall into specified noncitizen categories. CMS says the change begins Oct. 1, 2026 and notes exceptions such as continued federal matching for emergency medical conditions.

items.title: Federal Register: Comment deadline (May 1) for HUD proposal allowing optional work requirements/term limits in public housing and some Section 8 programs
items.summary: A HUD proposed rule would give Public Housing Agencies and certain multifamily owners the option to impose work requirements and term limits for some households in public housing and specified HUD-assisted programs. The public comment period ends May 1, 2026, meaning this is a near-term deadline for advocates and the public to weigh in.

items.title: USDA: SNAP FY 2026 benefit levels (COLA) include $298 max for 1-person households in the 48 states/DC
items.summary: USDA’s FY 2026 SNAP cost-of-living adjustments (effective Oct. 1, 2025–Sept. 30, 2026) set the maximum monthly allotment at $298 for a 1-person household and $994 for a 4-person household in the 48 states and DC. The same update lists a $744 maximum excess shelter deduction and a $24 minimum benefit for 1–2 person households.

items.title: USDA: WIC FY 2026 fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit amounts set at $26 (children), $48 (pregnant/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding)
items.summary: USDA issued FY 2026 WIC cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts that allow participants to buy fruits and vegetables. The memo lists $26/month for children, $48/month for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52/month for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants; it also lists a $3/month juice substitution amount.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI benefits rise 2.8% in 2026
items.summary: The Social Security Administration announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026, affecting about 75 million people. SSA says the increase begins with Social Security benefits payable in January 2026 and SSI payments starting Dec. 31, 2025.

items.title: Education Department proposes accountability rule that could cut off aid for low-earning programs; comments due May 20
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education published a proposed rule to hold colleges and programs accountable for low earnings outcomes, including removing federal student loan eligibility for programs whose typical graduates earn less than high school graduates. ED also notes that some low-performing programs could lose access to federal student loans and, in certain cases, Pell Grants; public comments are due May 20, 2026.

items.title: Federal Register: Education Department NPRM on Workforce Pell/STATS and earnings accountability; comments due May 20
items.summary: A Department of Education proposed rule titled
Accountability in Higher Education and Access Through Demand-Driven Workforce Pell
is open for public comment through May 20, 2026. The proposal references Workforce Pell and a new Student Tuition and Transparency System (STATS) alongside earnings accountability.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: 2025–26 FAFSA must be received by June 30, 2026
items.summary: Federal Student Aid’s 2025–26 FAFSA form materials state that applications must be received no later than June 30, 2026 for federal aid. State and school deadlines can be earlier, but June 30 is the final federal cutoff for submitting that year’s FAFSA.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)
